text: Maps are one of humanity's most enduring intellectual achievements, translating the vast and complex physical world into a form the mind can hold and reason about. They compress space into something navigable, turning the overwhelming scale of continents and oceans into a surface a person can trace with a fingertip. Beyond mere navigation, maps carry the values and assumptions of their makers, revealing who counted, what was considered important, and how power was arranged across territory. A map is never truly neutral; it is always an argument about the world disguised as a description of it.
